g.text: Partition healing demonstrates CAP theorem: during partition, both sides accept writes (Availability); after healing, anti-entropy converges state (Eventual Consistency)

div.p: Same version, different values Resolution: LWW with node ID tiebreaker Winner: Node4 (alphabetically higher)

Result: Eventual Consistency Achieved ✅ All nodes converged to same value ✅ Conflict resolved deterministically ✅ No data corruption Convergence time: ~3 anti-entropy rounds (~30 seconds with 10s interval) Trade-off: Partition A's write was lost (acceptable for AP systems)
Write during partition: Node1 writes key_x value = "from-A" version = 100

Concurrent write: Node4 writes key_x value = "from-B"
version = 100 ⚠️ CONFLICT! Same key, same version Different values

ol.li: Both detect: same key, same version

Apply LWW resolution:
Same version (100)
Tiebreaker: higher node ID
Winner: Node4 > Node1
